habitat: Fresh and salt waters: lakes; marshes; rivers; coasts; lagoons; swamps; and estuaries
story: An icon of the skies, the Common Comorant (Phalacrocorax carbo) is a species from the Phalacrocoracidae family. It thrives in Fresh and salt waters: lakes; marshes; rivers; coasts; lagoons; swamps; and estuaries, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Least Concern (LC), the Common Comorant is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Common Comorant helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Common Comorant, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
